responsibilities: Wires database, network monitoring, and UI components
file_insights.architecture: Offline-first with reactive UI updates and background sync

patterns_discovered.name: Repository Pattern
patterns_discovered.context: SyncStatusRepository manages sync state and provides reactive updates via Flow

patterns_discovered.name: Flow-based Reactive UI Updates
patterns_discovered.context: Uses lifecycleScope.launch to collect Flow updates and automatically cancel on activity destruction

patterns_discovered.name: WorkManager Periodic Task Scheduling
patterns_discovered.context: 15-minute periodic sync with network connectivity constraints and duplicate prevention via KEEP policy

patterns_discovered.name: Dependency Initialization in onCreate
patterns_discovered.context: AppDatabase, NetworkMonitor, and SyncStatusRepository created and initialized in onCreate method

patterns_discovered.name: Comprehensive Documentation Pattern
patterns_discovered.context: Extensive KDoc comments explaining architecture, responsibilities, and configuration rationale

gotchas_discovered.description: Uses findViewById instead of ViewBinding, which is less type-safe and more error-prone
Repository is created fresh on each onCreate, potentially causing memory leaks if activity is recreated multiple times
Flow collection in observeSyncState has no error handling or logging
NetworkMonitor is never cleaned up in onDestroy, could leak resources if it holds listeners
Relies only on 15-minute WorkManager interval; doesn't trigger immediate sync when network becomes available

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created MainActivity that observes SyncStatusRepository and manages background sync
accomplishments: Proper wiring of database, network monitoring, and UI components
Correct use of lifecycleScope for Flow collection with automatic cleanup
WorkManager periodic sync with network constraints properly configured
Clear separation of concerns with dedicated methods for sync observation and scheduling
Comprehensive documentation of architecture and component responsibilities

recommendations.recommendation: Implement dependency injection (Hilt/Dagger) or singleton pattern for SyncStatusRepository to avoid recreation and potential memory leaks
Replace findViewById with ViewBinding for type safety and compile-time verification
Add try-catch and logging to the Flow collection in observeSyncState method
Ensure NetworkMonitor properly cleans up resources in onDestroy; consider implementing Closeable interface
Consider registering a network callback to trigger immediate sync when network becomes available, rather than waiting for 15-minute interval
